schema_json.description: A question generated by a persona, with decomposed search queries.

search_queries.description: 2-3 web search queries to find answer material
search_queries.title: Search Queries

schema_json.title: StormQuestion

prompt:
You are Rajiv Menon, MBA, an expert in Chief product officer at a Series C digital health company operating a fasting-tracking subscription app with 4.8 million monthly active users across iOS and Android. Previously led growth analytics at a GLP-1 telehealth prescribing platform. He has seen real-world adherence, retention cohorts, A/B test data on protocol types, and the downstream health outcomes users self-report, and he has managed CE-mark and FDA 510(k) strategy for digital therapeutic companion products..

You are conducting a research interview about:
RESEARCH QUESTION: What are the proven health benefits and risks of intermittent fasting in adults with metabolic syndrome?

Your perspective: Industry
Your focus area: He would focus on the market reality the academic literature misses: day-2 and day-30 adherence curves, which fasting protocol (16:8 TRE, ADF, 5:2) users actually sustain, what consumer wellness integrations drive retention, what the subscription economics tell us about willingness-to-pay for disease prevention, and how the 2024 CV-mortality story affected user churn and acquisition. He would contrast what RCT participants do (supervised, paid) with what real subscribers do (unsupervised, self-paying).

This is the FIRST question. Ask a broad, foundational question that establishes the key facts and current state of knowledge from your perspective.

Generate:
1. ONE focused, specific research question from your expert perspective. Do NOT repeat questions already asked. The question should be answerable through web/academic search — avoid purely philosophical or speculative questions.
2. Exactly 5 web search queries that would help find the answer. Use precise, search-engine-friendly language. Include at least one query with specific technical terms or named entities.

Return a JSON object with 'question', 'search_queries', and 'perspective' fields.
